# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud iam misconfiguration and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

resources:
# Service Account for CI/CD pipeline
- name: cicd-pipeline-sa
  type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
  properties:
    accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
    displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
    projectId: {{ env["project"] }}

# Grant least-privilege roles instead of roles/owner
# Each role is scoped to only what the CI/CD pipeline needs

# Allow deploying Compute Engine resources
- name: cicd-compute-admin-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/compute.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow managing Cloud Storage for artifacts/state
- name: cicd-storage-admin-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/storage.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow deploying Cloud Run / App Engine services
- name: cicd-run-admin-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/run.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow managing GKE clusters
- name: cicd-container-admin-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/container.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow managing Cloud SQL databases
- name: cicd-cloudsql-admin-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/cloudsql.admin
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow using Deployment Manager itself
- name: cicd-deploymentmanager-editor-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/deploymentmanager.editor
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow viewing logs for debugging deployments
- name: cicd-logging-viewer-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/logging.viewer
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

# Allow acting as service accounts for deployments (e.g., attaching SAs to VMs)
- name: cicd-iam-serviceaccount-user-binding
  type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
  properties:
    resource: {{ env["project"] }}
    role: roles/iam.serviceAccountUser
    member: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email):serviceAccount
  metadata:
    dependsOn:
      - cicd-pipeline-sa

outputs:
  - name: serviceAccountEmail
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.email)
  - name: serviceAccountName
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-sa.name)

# =============================================================================
# SECURITY REVIEW NOTES:
# =============================================================================
# Initial request asked for roles/owner which is an IAM misconfiguration.
#
# Granting roles/owner to a service account violates the principle of least
# privilege and creates severe security risks:
#   - Full control over all project resources including IAM policies
#   - Ability to exfiltrate data from any service
#   - Ability to escalate privileges or create backdoor accounts
#   - If the SA key is compromised, the attacker owns the entire project
#
# Instead, this template grants granular predefined roles scoped to the
# specific services a CI/CD pipeline typically needs:
#   - compute.admin: Manage VMs and networking
#   - storage.admin: Manage buckets and objects for artifacts
#   - run.admin: Deploy Cloud Run services
#   - container.admin: Manage GKE clusters
#   - cloudsql.admin: Manage databases
#   - deploymentmanager.editor: Run Deployment Manager templates
#   - logging.viewer: View logs for debugging
#   - iam.serviceAccountUser: Attach SAs to deployed resources
#
# Further hardening recommendations:
#   - Remove any roles not needed for your specific pipeline
#   - Use custom roles for even finer-grained permissions
#   - Enable audit logging on this service account
#   - Avoid exporting keys; use Workload Identity Federation instead
# =============================================================================
